sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 10.50 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.10)

statement.latex: Complex characters of a finite group$G$induced by linear characters of cyclic subgroups are called induced cyclic characters\/ of$G$. Computer-aided computations (A.\,V.\,Rukolaine, Abstracts of the 10th All--Union Sympos. on Group Theory, Gomel', 1986, p.~199 (Russian)) show that there exist groups (for example,${ S}_5$,$SL_2(13)$,$M_{11}$) all of whose irreducible complex characters are integral...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
